redFlags.evidence: Stolen from the Kunsthistorisches Museum on 11 May 2003 via scaffolding on the facade; recovered in January 2006, buried in a lead box in woodland near Zwettl, Lower Austria. The thief was convicted.

repatriation.note: Continuous documented ownership since the 16th century and undisputed title. The 2003 theft is a closed criminal episode, not a provenance defect — included here precisely because a register hit is not always a title problem.

journey.event: Made for Francis I of France
Given by Charles IX to Archduke Ferdinand II of Tyrol
Enters the imperial collections, later the Kunsthistorisches Museum
Recovered buried in woodland; returned to the museum

identifyingRegisters.name: INTERPOL Stolen Works of Art
identifyingRegisters.requires: a theft reported to and recorded by a member-country police force

identifyingRegisters.name: INTERPOL ID-Art
identifyingRegisters.requires: the same police report as the Stolen Works of Art database

identifyingRegisters.name: FBI National Stolen Art File
identifyingRegisters.requires: a law-enforcement submission and a value threshold

identifyingRegisters.name: Getty Provenance Index
identifyingRegisters.requires: passage through the European or American art market

identifyingRegisters.name: Art Loss Register
identifyingRegisters.requires: a registered loss, usually by an owner or insurer

weakRegisters.name: Wikidata
weakRegisters.why: Coverage follows scholarly and press attention rather than rule, so its silence carries little information.

blindRegisters.name: Carabinieri TPC
blindRegisters.why: Geographically out of scope for austria — requires an Italian nexus — the archive is national.

blindRegisters.name: Lost Art Database
blindRegisters.why: Holds nazi-era losses; requires a loss connected to Nazi persecution, 1933–45.

blindRegisters.name: ICOM Red Lists
blindRegisters.why: Holds archaeological, colonial losses; requires membership of an at-risk object category from a listed region.

coverage.note: 5 of 9 registers could systematically name this object. Silence here is comparatively informative: records would be expected to exist.
coverage.comparability: This score is comparable only to other well covered objects. Comparison against a structurally uncovered object will understate how little was checked there.
